title: sk-code verifier reports Python .sh entrypoints as shell-style drift
claim: The alignment verifier classifies all .sh files as shell, so the two Python shebang scripts with .sh names are reported as missing shell shebang and strict mode, creating recurring non-blocking noise around the same comment-hygiene surface.
recommendation: Rename the Python scripts to .py and update callers, add shell wrappers, or teach the verifier to honor shebang over extension for known entrypoints.
